_doc: Machine-readable manifest of every HARD / MUST rule in spec/AGENTS*.md. It drives hook wiring, rule-specific opportunity/outcome review, and CI anchor drift checks. Telemetry never edits the spec automatically: zero or high hits require an eligible/evaluated denominator plus operator review. Update this manifest in the same edit as a HARD rule. enforcement: self | hook | external | both.
_live_sections_doc: spec_section values emitted or observed by a built and registered hook. A live section with zero rule-specific eligible/evaluated opportunities is no-opportunity, not a demotion signal. Update this array when a hook starts or stops emitting/observing a section.
_demote_policy_doc: Optional per-rule governance policy used by scripts/rules.js. A standard live core rule becomes a demotion candidate only after the minimum distinct eligible and evaluated sessions with zero enforcement outcomes. deterrence rules are never demoted from telemetry alone. governance_parent marks an overlapping subclause whose promotion/demotion signal is inherited from one disjoint parent rule, preventing duplicate recommendations from the same telemetry bucket. Extended rules have no lower prompt tier and receive hook-value-review instead. Every signal remains operator input, not an automatic edit.

rules.name: rm -rf $VAR safety (immutable)
rules.note: Immutable §8. First blocking hook to port (pre-bash-safety-check.sh).

rules.name: Unknown-origin script execution (immutable)
rules.note: Covers curl, wget, fetch, HTTPie, and aria2 downloads executed in the same command or by a later command in the same session, including bounded nested-shell downloads. Cross-tool blocking requires the exact recorded output path to exist and resolves later relative execution against the event cwd. Unpinned npx is a separate non-safety advisory and never contributes to this immutable rule's telemetry.

rules.name: No plaintext secrets in code/logs/commits (immutable)
rules.note: Hook scans outgoing git commit/diff for key/token patterns; self-enforced elsewhere.

rules.name: No unbounded DELETE/UPDATE; destructive schema/data operations require a rollback plan
rules.note: DELETE/UPDATE require a bounded predicate and remain prohibited even with authorization. DROP/TRUNCATE use the separate hard-AUTH + reviewed backup/rollback rule because those statements have no WHERE clause.

rules.name: No unbounded recursive traversal of home/config dirs (immutable)
rules.section_anchor: unbounded recursive traversal of home/config dirs
rules.note: Self-enforced — same rationale as §8-sql-no-where: reliable mechanical detection at the bash-hook layer is infeasible (legitimate recursive rg/find/ls -R commands are common). A narrow PreToolUse detector (unscoped traversal of ~/.codex ~/.claude ~ without -maxdepth) is a PLANNED addition, NOT yet built; the manifest must not claim a hook that does not exist. Flip to enforcement 'both' + add §8-home-traversal to live_sections only when that detector actually ships.

rules.name: Verify-before-claim V1-V4
rules.note: V4 sandbox-artifact disposal partially hookable via a Stop residue/disposal check; V1-V3 self-enforced.

rules.name: Sandbox-artifact disposal (V4)
rules.note: Stop-hook advisory on mkdtemp/scratch residue newer than session baseline; excludes Codex runtime-owned synthetic mount targets because recency alone does not prove task ownership.

rules.name: Iron Law #1: no change without pre-change evidence (L2+)
Iron Law #2: no done without fresh evidence
rules.note: Never deferrable (not even under EMERGENCY), and never demotable — a foundational Iron Law stays core regardless of hit count (demote_policy 'deterrence': 0 hits = no unanchored claim arose, not dilution). transcript-structure-scan.sh (Stop) realizes the 'evidence-anchor presence' observer: a completed-fix claim (fixed / resolved / 修复 / 解决了) with NO evidence fingerprint anywhere (test count / file:line / exit code / commit hash / failing-state token / inline `code`) records under §6-iron-law-2 + queues an advisory. Complements §10-V banned-vocab (which fires on the PRESENCE of a bad phrasing) by catching the ABSENCE of any anchor behind a fix claim; the general 'done without evidence' case the heuristic can't see stays self-enforced.

rules.name: Iron Law #3: no fix without root cause (L2+)
Bugfix anchor + banned phrasings (missing-evidence tokens)
rules.note: Shares the banned-vocab pattern set AND the §10-V telemetry bucket with §10-specificity by design — the two are governed as one banned-vocab signal (per-rule attribution is intentionally merged; a §10-V hit is not split between bugfix-anchor vs specificity). Hook scans commit prose + Stop transcript.

rules.name: Destructive smoke test before live run (§8.V3)
MEMORY.md read-the-file at ship/destructive/L3
rules.section_anchor: MUST read the linked file before proceeding (HARD at ship/destructive/L3)
rules.note: Hook fires on git push/merge/publish Bash; requires successful tool-output evidence for the selected MEMORY.md index and a linked local memory file when the index contains local Markdown links.

rules.name: Exit archival (HARD) — distill dead-ends to memory/ before abnormal exit
Session-exit mid-SPINE (HARD) — un-validated work is not 'Completed'
rules.note: Stop hook session-exit-checkpoint.sh records an advisory (once per unvalidated streak) when a mutation is not followed by test/lint/typecheck/build evidence, and drops a per-session state flag; git commit/push are not validation. session-start-check.sh surfaces a prior session's leftover flag at the next SessionStart. The paused-task file itself stays self-enforced (the hook observes + nudges, it never writes into the user's repo).

rules.name: Memory hygiene — index/file drift + missing verified-header (§7)
rules.section_anchor: Index hygiene: entry stale + un-re-verifiable after one refresh attempt
rules.note: Stop hook mem-audit.sh audits the resolved MEMORY.md (the same hook_find_memory_file the hint routes off): index_orphan (index line → missing memory/*.md) and file_orphan (memory/*.md with no index line) queue one advisory per memory-dir per 24h; missing_header (a memory/*.md lacking the mandated `verified: <date> | source:` line) records telemetry only, never surfaced — soft decay is measured, not nagged. Depth-1 dir read (§8); fail-open. The archival / re-verify actions themselves stay self-enforced.

rules.name: Known-red remote branch observer before push
rules.note: Hook blocks a shared-branch push only when the latest observable GitHub run is conclusively red. Full/local/fresh validation remains self-enforced by §E3-ship-checklist.

rules.name: Four-section REPORT order (Done -> Not done -> Failed -> Uncertain)
rules.note: Stop transcript-structure scan checks section presence + order in four-section reports.

rules.name: Honesty rules (yes/no first, no evaluative framing, no hedging)
rules.note: transcript-structure-scan.sh (Stop) observes the hedging clause: an Uncertain section that hedges (may / could / might / 可能 / 或许) without a because / 因为 / 由于 justification records under §10-honesty + queues an advisory. Scoped to the Uncertain-section tail so legitimate hedges elsewhere don't trip it. The yes/no-first and no-evaluative-framing clauses stay self-enforced. Foundational honesty rule — never demotable (demote_policy 'deterrence': 0 hits = no hedge arose, not dilution).

rules.name: Specificity + banned vocab (absolute numbers / baseline-anchored ratios)
rules.note: Hook enforces banned-vocab subset on git commit messages; Stop scan covers assistant prose; self-enforced elsewhere. Shares the §10-V telemetry bucket with §6-bugfix-anchor by design — a §10-V hit is governed as one merged banned-vocab signal, not attributed per-rule.

rules.name: Plan before execute (L2+, MUST) via update_plan
Recurrence check (L1 bugfix) via git log --grep
rules.note: Git history is the signature store — the recurrence detector runs without extended loaded.

rules.name: Hard AUTH gates — semantic authorization, with explicit ship-intent reuse
rules.note: AUTH is operation-scoped and independent of LEVEL; L3 alone is not a gate. Explicit commit-and-release intent authorizes the standard current-repo ship closure without a redundant prompt; other irreversible, external-state, CI, production, data, security, global-config/routing, and out-of-scope ship operations still ask.

rules.name: Level hard-upgrades (API/auth/payment, migration/infra, released-artifact, LLM-visible metadata)
Preflight git status before edits (L1+)
End-of-task sweep (L2+) — no orphan files
rules.note: Stop residue-audit advisory on ~/.codex/tmp growth; the git-status sweep is self-enforced.

rules.name: Parallel-path completeness (L2+) — enumerate + verify every branch
rules.section_anchor: enumerate every path before edit, verify each after
rules.note: §9 Beyond-green-tests parallel-path rule (fallback / feature flag / default match arm / early return / multi-dispatch): enumerate every branch before edit, verify each after; main-path green + silent siblings is not evidence. Self-enforced — 'did you check every branch' has no reliable bash-hook detector (semantic, needs code understanding); the manifest entry makes it a first-class governed rule (rules.js review cadence) instead of un-tracked prose. The rule text already lived in AGENTS.md §9; C2 adds a manifest row, no spec prose.

rules.name: Load the extended spec on every declared trigger
rules.note: The rule predates this manifest row; reverse HARD/MUST drift coverage now prevents it from becoming ungoverned prose.

rules.name: Never disable SSL or certificate verification
rules.note: Immutable §8; dynamic command/config forms make a low-false-positive generic hook infeasible, so enforcement remains self.

rules.name: Never commit .env or private-key files
rules.note: secrets-scan blocks high-confidence secret filenames in the effective commit while allowing documented .env templates; non-Bash and ambiguous key formats remain self-enforced.

rules.name: Never edit .git internals directly
rules.note: Immutable §8; Git plumbing and worktree layouts make path-only interception unsafe, so enforcement remains self.

rules.name: Emergency deferral requires a root-cause and regression-test follow-up
rules.section_anchor: follow-up task MUST backfill root cause + regression test

rules.name: Skill descriptions front-load trigger, scope, and non-trigger boundaries
rules.section_anchor: Description MUST front-load trigger words, scope, AND non-trigger cases

rules.name: Plugin MUST wording never overrides the core spec
rules.section_anchor: 'MUST invoke' wording does not override core §4 level routing

rules.name: L3 phase gate (no phase N+1 while phase N un-validated)
rules.section_anchor: no phase N+1 while phase N is un-validated

rules.name: Ship/release checklist gate order
rules.section_anchor: Gate order — a red item stops the pipeline
rules.note: The full 9-item gate order includes local/full/fresh validation, explicit-authorization reuse, default-branch integration, artifact verification, and merged release-branch cleanup. The sibling §E3-ship-baseline hook is only a known-red remote observer and owns its telemetry section.

rules.name: L3 evidence ladder (claim strength <= tier actually run)
Skill/prompt/MCP-description authoring uses risk-tiered LLM-visible metadata levels
Mid-SPINE turn-yield — silent mid-cycle stop + next-turn done-claim = Iron Law #2 evasion
rules.note: Extended §E8. Per-turn analog of core §7 Session-exit mid-SPINE: yielding a turn mid-cycle (before VALIDATE) then claiming done next turn asserts unrun steps = Iron Law #2 violation. Self-enforced; the always-on protection is core Iron Law #2 + §7 session-exit, this elaborates the specific evasion. An optional retrospective Stop observer overlaps B1 (session-exit-checkpoint) + roadmap C4 (iron-law-2 evidence-fingerprint), so measurement is deferred there rather than adding a third overlapping Stop hook.
